statement: A uniform disk of mass 4 kg and radius 0.3 m is free to rotate about a fixed axle through its center. A tangential force of 12 N is applied at the rim. Find the angular acceleration of the disk. The moment of inertia of a disk about its center is I = ½·m·r².
common_student_errors: Using I = m·r² (solid sphere formula) instead of I = ½·m·r² for a disk
Forgetting to multiply the force by the radius to get the torque: using τ = F instead of τ = F·r
Confusing angular acceleration α (rad/s²) with linear acceleration a (m/s²)
